article_title: Uptime Institute's 2026 Outage Analysis Finds Data Center Failures Getting Rarer but Costlier, With Power Still the Leading Cause

content_preview.title: Uptime Institute's 2026 Outage Analysis Finds Data Center Failures Getting Rarer but Costlier, With Power Still the Leading Cause
content_preview.summary: Uptime's 2026 report marks a fifth straight year of declining outage frequency, but 57% of major outages now cost over $100,000 and power remains the top cause.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Data center outages are becoming less frequent but more expensive, according to Uptime Intelligence's Annual Outage Analysis Report 2026. The report marks the fifth consecutive year that outage frequency on a per-site basis has declined, as reported by [Network World](https://www.networkworld.com/article/4171277/network-outages-power-failures-strain-data-center-resiliency.html). At the same time, [57% of respondents said their most recent major outage cost more than $100,000](https:...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong, numerically dense News+Analysis piece (727 words) on Uptime's 2026 outage report. Carefully distinguishes survey-based findings from open questions.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200). source-2 (Network World) confirms — verbatim — the fifth-consecutive-year frequency decline, 'half of operators … down from 74% in 2020', 45% power, 23% networking, 92% human-error (31% major / 31% moderate), and the three direct quotes from Andy Lawrence, Daniel Bizo, and Amber Villegas-Williamson with correct speaker attribution. source-1 (Data Centre Solutions) confirms the 57% / $100,000 figure, the 'Power remains the leading cause' quote, and the fiber/connectivity quote. source-0 (Uptime Intelligence) confirms the 'second year in a row, one in five … more than $1 million' fact (also carried verbatim in DCS).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every percentage, dollar figure, and year checks out against the cited outlet. The 'second consecutive year / one in five / $1 million' claim cited to Uptime Intelligence is supported there ('second year in a row') and corroborated verbatim in DCS — correctly attributed, no fabrication. BusinessWire was bot-blocked and was correctly excluded rather than cited.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; the Analysis section is clearly labeled and grounded in the reported trends.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — no prior Uptime Institute or data-center-outage coverage in the archive.
recommendations: Added intelligence.uptimeinstitute.com (the primary report source) and datacentre.solutions to the allowlist.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-verified data-heavy submission with no unsupported specifics. APPROVE.
